description: Canonical, committed LoCoMo v5 trio-strict reproducibility artifacts. `node benchmarks/locomo/rescore.mjs` re-derives the headline numbers below offline from these files with ZERO API calls. This is the regression baseline for the substrate's benchmark claim.
run: v5 — Claude Opus 4.7 as benchmark subject, trio-strict re-judge (2026-05-21, v2 post-parser-fix)

sample.stratification: 80 per category across [multi-hop, temporal, open-ended, single-hop]; adversarial (category 5) excluded
sample.algorithm: xorshift32(seed=42) + Fisher-Yates per bucket; bucket order [1,2,3,4]; sort by instance_id asc

judges.trio_strict_rule: CORRECT only when ALL THREE judges return CORRECT (logical AND). A row where any judge fails to parse is excluded (counted as not-correct; denominator stays 320).
judges.trio_majority_rule: CORRECT when >=2 of 3 judges return CORRECT; parse-failure rows excluded (counted as not-correct).

trio-judgments-v5-retrieval.v2.jsonl.role: canonical per-row trio judgments (the rescore input)

cell-retrieval-v5-claude.jsonl.role: provenance — the v5 retrieval answers that were judged

sample-cells-23-N320.jsonl.role: provenance — the N=320 stratified question sample (the test set)
